title: Mecodium crispatoalatum
taxonomy: Mecodium crispatoalatum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Mecodium, which is a group of epiphytic and terrestrial ferns. Mecodium crispatoalatum is native to Papua New Guinea and is often found in humid, tropical forests. The classification of this plant is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its leathery fronds and distinctive leaf margins. As a member of the Polypodiaceae family, Mecodium crispatoalatum is closely related to other species of ferns in this family.
